resources.string: Choose the language used by MOTO-HUB
MOTO-HUB follows the phone language by default. Choose a language below to override it for this app.
Language, seamless resume, and app behavior
Check GitHub releases automatically, at most once every 24 hours
Android Auto can resume after a long T-Box interruption with the screen off
Allow Android Auto to resume in the background when the bike comes back; permission required on first enable
Lower bitrate, less heat and network load.
Current MOTO-HUB quality and recommended default.
Higher bitrate for crisper maps and text.
Adapt bitrate and frame rate to phone temperature and Wi-Fi quality.
30 FPS with the selected video quality.
Stable 24 FPS with the selected video quality.
20 FPS for reduced heat and battery use.
Keep MOTO-HUB automatic orientation based on the learned T-Box geometry.
Sharper HD landscape source with higher phone load.
Sharper HD portrait source with higher phone load.
Use the full Android Auto source; the T-Box projection area adds no AA margins.
Advertise the per-motorcycle TFT Safe Margins to Android Auto.
Distances and speeds in km and km/h.
Distances and speeds in mi and mph.
Bias toward back roads and away from motorways.
Streaming status for the motorcycle display
Session active. Open MOTO-HUB for live projection status.
GPS telemetry and dashboard streaming are active.
Everything on this page controls the projection currently shown on the motorcycle.
The value is restored when handlebar capture is switched off.
Use the motorcycle buttons without looking down. The active projection receives the commands first.
No diagnostic events have been recorded.
Persistent events from Wi-Fi, T-Box, mirroring, encoder, and Android Auto.
Check T-Box and cellular routes without starting a VPN or changing the default network.
Start a projection to populate the log.
Add a motorcycle by scanning its T-Box QR code.
Choose how the complete Android Auto image is fitted to the TFT.
Exclude pixels occupied by the motorcycle UI. These values are stored for this
Full-tank range on a fill-up. Powers the fuel-range warning in navigation.
Override the automatic profile detection for this motorcycle. Use only if the
The Wi-Fi password is stored securely on this phone.
The name is only used inside MOTO-HUB.
The saved connection profile and its photo will be removed from MOTO-HUB.
Could not derive a peer IP - this network may not have a usable route yet.
If EasyConn discovery keeps failing, briefly reconnect to this T-Box and probe
Another EasyConn app can keep the T-Box link occupied even after it leaves the foreground.
Choose what the Ride Dashboard shows for navigation.
Scan the T-Box QR code to save the network credentials automatically.
Exact destination pin used for routing. Tap the map to change it.
Guidance to your destination will end.
Navigation will start either way. Open the Ride Dashboard on the motorcycle TFT?
No favorites yet. Save one from a route preview or an arrival screen.
Not every address number is mapped in OpenStreetMap yet, so the pin
Save a destination as a favorite from a route preview.
Auto detects DIRECT- networks. Choose Wi-Fi Direct for a dashboard that is a P2P Group Owner.
Some motorcycles don’t show a pairing QR code on the dash. If you already know
Uses this phone’s GPS - no T-Box needed. Same renderer and heading-up/zoom
Choose image detail and how MOTO-HUB balances smoothness, heat, battery, and Wi-Fi load.
Turn-by-turn routing uses your own Stadia Maps API key, never a
GPS logging works with the phone locked
Recording was too short to keep
Ride recordings need at least 100 m, 15 s of movement, and two GPS points.
The GPS trace and all recorded statistics will be permanently removed.
No APK asset attached to this release.
Show the complete image with black side bars when needed.
Use the whole TFT and keep all content visible with slight stretching.
Use the whole TFT without stretching; crop the edges when aspect ratios differ.
Adjust the phone media level while handlebar capture is active.
Choose the day/night style used by Android Auto.
Customize double taps, holds, and Android Auto commands.
Destinations available to one-press handlebar navigation.
Choose widgets for each side panel
Endpoint, geometry, protocol, and feature flags
Exclude pixels occupied by the motorcycle UI
How the complete Android Auto image fits the TFT
Draw recorded points on the dashboard map.
Keep the TFT active while reducing phone distraction.
Open the on-screen Android Auto controls.
View Android Auto and interact from your phone.
Review your route before starting guidance.
Auto-recover stalled Android Auto and Ride Dashboard streams
Connect and discover T-Box when app opens
Draw detected curved segments in a distinct color on the TFT map
Encoder detail and power mode for all streams
Full CLIENT_INFO, every candidate profile’s score, unknown command
Master switch for the diagnostic log. Off means nothing is recorded
Review, copy, share, or clear events
Routes through FOSSGIS’s free public Valhalla server instead of
See the TFT render on this phone, using its own GPS - no T-Box needed
Show forecast conditions at your destination in the route preview
Show minutes to the nearest sunrise/sunset golden hour
Start GPS logging with projection sessions
T-Box discovery, Wi-Fi binding, cellular routes
Use focus and handlebar controls even when the T-Box reports a touch display
Warn when a route would exceed your motorcycle’s tank range (set in Garage)
Your own routing API key for turn-by-turn
GPS ride recording and trip statistics
Album cover, current track and progress
Arc speedometer, bearing and GNSS lock status
Battery, charging, connectivity, temperature and motion sensors
Current conditions, wind, humidity via Open-Meteo
Elevation profile, climb rate and GPS accuracy
GNSS satellite visibility and fix status
Heading, sun altitude and rider-relative sun position
Large, glove-friendly actions for the active dashboard.
Manage guidance, search a new destination, or resume your route.
Native turn-by-turn navigation rendered on the TFT
Next turn, ETA, sun position, daylight remaining
Pad fullscreen — screen stays on. Tap Exit to leave.
Search a destination, pick a favorite, or start from a saved route.
Send navigation and selection commands to Android Auto.
Start Android Auto to enable the controller.
Start the dashboard to enable these actions.
Trip distance, duration, average speed and speed profile
Two next-track presses inside the window
Two presses inside the double-tap window
Unable to save the motorcycle photo
Allow MOTO-HUB notifications to keep streaming visible and controllable.
Allow Nearby devices and Location to detect the T-Box Wi-Fi network.
Android is requesting a connection to %1$s.
Camera permission is required to read the T-Box QR code.
Connection cancelled. You can try again at any time.
Network connected. Searching for the EasyConn service.
No known phone location yet; move outdoors and retry.
Pair a motorcycle in Garage before starting navigation.
Permission granted. Starting the capture session.
Profile saved. Connect to the T-Box network and start discovery.
QR code scanned, but the profile was not saved: %1$s
Ride Dashboard streaming is active on the motorcycle TFT.
Set up the motorcycle network to get started.
Stop the current mirroring or Android Auto session before opening the Ride Dashboard from here.
Streaming active to the motorcycle TFT.
T-Box QR code scanned and saved. %1$s is ready to connect.
T-Box connection lost. Reconnect to the motorcycle network.
T-Box found. You can now choose an app or screen to share.
That search is too long. Try a shorter search.
Connect a motorcycle before customizing its dashboard.
Location and notifications are required while recording a trip
Unable to open official CFMOTO app settings
MOTO-HUB starts Google Android Auto, not MotoPlay.
The official CFMOTO MotoPlay/EasyConn app can still take over the T-Box connection while it is in the background. For reliable projection, force-stop it in Android settings before continuing.
